videos.hook_text: Meet the bird that circles the ENTIRE planet... almost twice a year.
videos.notes: record-chasing structure (distance -> speed -> altitude -> endurance) escalating to the frigatebird's 48h no-land flight; every mechanic anchors value to a real-world yardstick (Earth's circumference, Everest's height, a day/night cycle) instead of a bare bar.

timeline.detail: circles the planet almost twice a year
earth-lap-trails: tern/falcon/sandpiper arcs sweep around a real Earth image
stoop-dive-speedometer: peregrine/eagle/gyrfalcon dive a vertical mph ruler
everest-altitude-ladder: geese/swan/vulture perch at real altitude over an Everest image
endurance-daynight-bar: frigatebird's 48h bar fills past two day/night cycles

videos.hook_text: Birds fly thousands of miles with zero map, zero GPS — here's how they actually do it.
videos.notes: same core bird-record numbers as birds-migrate-insanely-far but reframed around the HOW (instinct/navigation) rather than the records, to avoid two videos feeling like reruns of the same data; ends by inverting the hook's question (no map needed at all).

timeline.detail: zero map, zero GPS — how do they do it
mileage-runway: tern/falcon/cuckoo mileage unrolls on a ticked ruler
gauge-trio-sweep: three speedometer dials sweep to real top speeds
calendar-creep: arrival marker drags 20 real days earlier on a calendar
instinct-dial-pie: magnetic/visual/olfactory wedges split the real navigation shares
